stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 30 patients. The sample mean is 231.7 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 83.0 mmHg. However, 2 of the 30 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 28 observations have a mean of 226.5 mmHg and standard deviation of 55.6 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 231.7, 95% CI = [200.7, 262.7]. Without outliers: mean = 226.5, 95% CI = [205.5, 247.5]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [200.7, 262.7]. Point estimate: 229.1 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [200.7, 262.7].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
